tasks.name: Identify the most recent Kubernetes CI/CD DevOps project and record its title
tasks.description: Find the latest DevOps project that uses both Kubernetes and a CI/CD tool, and save its exact title into the evaluation notes field. Success is achieved when the 'Most relevant project' field contains the title of the newest project matching DevOps + Kubernetes + CI/CD criteria.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click on the 'Projects' link in the main navigation bar to open the projects listing page.
In the filters sidebar, click the 'Category' dropdown and select the 'DevOps' checkbox.
In the filters sidebar, open the 'Technologies' or 'Tags' section and select both 'Kubernetes' and 'CI/CD' checkboxes.
Click the 'Sort by' dropdown above the project list and choose 'Last updated (Newest first)'.
Click on the first project card in the sorted results list (this represents the most recently updated matching project) to open its detail page.
On the project detail page, scroll down until the 'Project title' or main heading is fully visible at the top of the content area.
Select the full project title text and use the interface control provided (for example, a 'Copy title' button next to the heading) to copy the title.
Open the 'Recruiter notes' or 'Evaluation' panel from the sidebar or header and click into the input labeled 'Most relevant project'.
Paste the copied project title into the 'Most relevant project' field using the standard paste action.

tasks.name: Prepare a contact message referencing two microservices projects using Go or Node.js
tasks.description: Find two microservices-related projects where the primary backend language is either Go or Node.js, then send a contact message that mentions both project titles and a preferred interview date. Success is achieved when the contact form is submitted with a message containing the exact two chosen project titles and a date string.
steps: From any page, click the 'Projects' link in the main navigation to open the projects listing.
In the search bar above the project list, type 'microservices' and press Enter to filter the projects.
In the 'Languages' filter section, select the 'Go' and 'Node.js' checkboxes so that only projects using Go or Node.js are shown.
Scroll through the filtered project list and click on the first project card to open its detail page, then note the exact project title shown in the main heading.
Use the browser's back navigation or the 'Back to projects' link to return to the projects listing, then click on the second project card in the list and note its exact title as well.
Open the main navigation and click on the 'Contact' link to go to the contact form page.
In the contact form, click into the 'Email' field and type 'recruiter@example-company.com'.
Click into the 'Subject' field and type 'Microservices candidate inquiry'.

In the 'Message' textarea, type a message that includes both noted project titles and a specific date, for example: 'I am interested in your microservices experience, especially the projects "[First project title]" and "[Second project title]". I would like to schedule a 30-minute interview on 15 August 2024.' Replace the placeholders with the exact titles you noted.
Click the 'Send message' or 'Submit' button at the bottom of the contact form to send the message.

tasks.name: Shortlist exactly three AWS-related skills with proficiency 4 or higher in DevOps/Cloud categories
tasks.description: Locate skills related to AWS within DevOps or Cloud categories that have a proficiency level of at least 4 out of 5, and add exactly three of them to the shortlist. Success is achieved when the shortlist contains three and only three AWS skills that meet the category and proficiency criteria.
steps: From the homepage, click the 'Skills' option in the main navigation menu to open the skills overview page.
In the skills filters panel, open the 'Category' dropdown and select the 'DevOps' and 'Cloud' checkboxes so that both categories are active.
In the 'Platform/Provider' filter section, select the 'AWS' checkbox to show only AWS-related skills.
Locate the proficiency slider or rating filter and set the minimum proficiency value to 4 (on a 1–5 scale), leaving the maximum at 5.
Scroll through the filtered list of skills and identify the first three skills that match the AWS filter and are displayed with a proficiency of 4 or 5.
For the first matching skill, click the 'Add to shortlist' button or star icon next to its name.
For the second matching skill, click the same 'Add to shortlist' or star control to include it in the shortlist.
For the third matching skill, again click the 'Add to shortlist' or star control to add it.
Click the 'View shortlist' button or open the 'Shortlist' tab/panel to navigate to the shortlist view that shows the selected skills.

tasks.name: Create a reading list with one short and one long recent DevOps blog post
tasks.description: From the blog, select two DevOps-tagged posts published in or after 2023: one with an estimated reading time of 7 minutes or less, and one with 12 minutes or more. Add both posts to the reading list. Success is achieved when the reading list contains exactly these two posts matching the date, tag, and reading-time criteria.
steps: Click the 'Blog' link in the main navigation bar to open the blog listing page.
In the tag filter area, click on the 'DevOps' tag to show only DevOps-related posts.
Use the 'Published date' filter to set the start date to '01 Jan 2023' and leave the end date as the default or current date.
Locate the 'Reading time' filter or slider and adjust it to display posts with any reading time, then apply the filter so you can see the estimated reading time on each post card.
Scroll through the filtered posts and identify the first post whose displayed reading time is 7 minutes or less; click on that post card to open its detail page.
On the post detail page, click the 'Add to reading list' or bookmark button located near the title to add this short post to your reading list.
Use the navigation control (e.g., 'Back to blog') to return to the blog listing and scroll until you find the first post whose displayed reading time is 12 minutes or more.
Click on that long post card to open its detail page, then click the 'Add to reading list' or bookmark button to add this long post to your reading list.
Open the header menu or sidebar and click the 'Reading list' or 'Bookmarks' link to navigate to the page showing saved posts.

tasks.name: Record the earliest start year of any DevOps or SRE role in the experience timeline
tasks.description: Examine the experience timeline to find the earliest start year among positions labeled as DevOps or SRE, and record that year in an evaluation form. Success is achieved when the 'Earliest DevOps/SRE year' field contains the correct four-digit year from the oldest such role.
steps: From the homepage, click on the 'About' or 'Experience' link in the main navigation to open the profile and experience page.
Scroll down to the 'Experience' or 'Career Timeline' section where past roles are listed chronologically.
If there is a role-type filter above the timeline, open it and select only the 'DevOps' and 'SRE' checkboxes so that other role types are hidden.
Scan the timeline entries and identify the role with the earliest (oldest) start year among the visible DevOps and SRE positions.
Mentally note the four-digit start year of this earliest DevOps/SRE role (for example, '2016').
Scroll to or open the 'Recruiter evaluation' or 'Notes' panel accessible from the same page (or via a button near the experience section).
In the field labeled 'Earliest DevOps/SRE year', click into the input box and type the four-digit year you noted from the earliest role.
Click the 'Save notes' or 'Save evaluation' button associated with the evaluation panel to store the entered year.

tasks.name: Compare two most recent production Terraform-on-AWS projects
tasks.description: Find projects that use Terraform on AWS in a production environment, then add the two most recently started such projects to the comparison view. Success is achieved when the comparison view displays exactly the two newest matching projects.
steps: Navigate to the homepage and click the 'Projects' item in the main navigation to open the projects listing.
In the filters sidebar, open the 'Technologies' section and select the 'Terraform' checkbox.
In the same or a separate 'Cloud provider' filter section, select the 'AWS' checkbox.
Open the 'Environment' or 'Deployment' filter dropdown and select the 'Production' checkbox so that only production projects are shown.
Use the 'Sort by' dropdown and choose 'Start date (Newest first)' to order projects from the most recently started to the oldest.
Click on the first project card in the sorted list and then click the 'Add to comparison' button or toggle on the project detail or card.
Return to the project listing (if you navigated away) and click on the second project card in the sorted list, then click the 'Add to comparison' button or toggle for this second project.
Click the 'Compare projects' button or open the 'Comparison' tab/panel available near the top or bottom of the projects page to navigate to the project comparison view.

tasks.name: Generate a tailored resume view for a Senior DevOps role focused on CI/CD and Observability in Germany
tasks.description: Use the tailored resume builder to generate a resume view targeting a 'Senior DevOps Engineer' position in Germany, emphasizing CI/CD and Observability skills, and including only experience from the last 4 years. Success is achieved when the generated resume view is displayed with the specified role, location, skills emphasis, and time range applied.
steps: From any page, open the main navigation menu and click on the 'Tailored Resume' or 'Resume Builder' link to access the resume customization page.
In the 'Target role' dropdown, select 'Senior DevOps Engineer' from the list of available roles.
In the 'Location' or 'Region' field, click the dropdown or input and select or type 'Germany'.
Locate the 'Experience timeframe' or 'Include experience from' control and set it to 'Last 4 years' using the provided dropdown or slider.
In the 'Focus skills' or 'Highlight areas' section, search for and select 'CI/CD' and 'Observability' so that both are marked as focus areas (for example, by checking their checkboxes).
If there is a 'Remote preference' toggle, set it to 'Open to remote' or select the option that indicates remote-friendly roles.
Click the 'Generate resume' or 'Update preview' button to apply the chosen role, location, timeframe, and skill focus.
After the preview updates, click the 'Print-friendly view' or 'Full-screen resume' button to open the tailored resume in its dedicated view.

tasks.name: Subscribe to the newsletter with topics based on available Kubernetes and Monitoring articles
tasks.description: Identify at least one blog post tagged 'Kubernetes' and one tagged 'Monitoring', then subscribe to the newsletter using a work email, selecting 'Weekly' frequency and the DevOps and Cloud topics. Success is achieved when the subscription form is submitted with the specified email, frequency, and topics.
steps: Click the 'Blog' item in the main navigation to open the blog listing page.
In the tags area, click the 'Kubernetes' tag to filter and display Kubernetes-related posts, and scroll enough to confirm that at least one post appears in the list.
Clear the tag filter or navigate back to the full blog list, then click the 'Monitoring' tag to filter and display Monitoring-related posts, scrolling to reveal at least one such post in the list.
Open the main navigation or footer and click on the 'Newsletter' or 'Subscribe' link to go to the newsletter subscription page.
In the 'Email' input field, type 'hiring.manager@company.com'.
In the 'Frequency' dropdown, select the 'Weekly' option.
In the 'Topics' or 'Interests' section, select the 'DevOps' and 'Cloud' checkboxes so that both topics are chosen.
Click the 'Subscribe' or 'Sign up' button at the bottom of the form to submit the newsletter subscription.

entities.name: Page
entities.description: Static pages that make up the portfolio site (Home, Projects, Blog, etc.)

entities.name: NavigationLink
entities.description: Links shown in header and footer navigation

fields.name: page_filename
fields.description: Filename of the target Page, e.g. 'projects.html'

entities.name: Profile
entities.description: Core personal profile for the engineer used on Home, About, and Resume

fields.name: primary_specializations
fields.description: Array of specialization labels, e.g. ['Software Engineering', 'DevOps', 'SRE']

entities.name: ProjectCategory
entities.description: High-level project categories such as DevOps, Software Engineering, SRE

entities.name: Project
entities.description: Portfolio projects displayed in the Projects listing, detail, and comparison views

fields.name: role_title
fields.description: Role in the project, e.g. 'DevOps Engineer'

fields.name: organization
fields.description: Client or employer name if applicable

fields.name: technologies
fields.description: Array of technology names, e.g. ['Kubernetes', 'Terraform', 'GitHub Actions']

fields.name: languages
fields.description: Array of backend language identifiers, matching primary_backend_language values

fields.name: is_microservices
fields.description: True if the project is microservices-based

fields.name: has_ci_cd
fields.description: True if CI/CD tooling was a primary focus

fields.name: is_featured
fields.description: Featured on home page or highlights

entities.name: ProjectComparisonSelection
entities.description: Current set of projects selected for comparison

fields.name: project_ids
fields.description: Array of Project.id values selected for comparison

entities.name: SkillCategory
entities.description: Categories for skills, e.g., DevOps, Cloud, Software Engineering

entities.name: Skill
entities.description: Individual skills with proficiency, category, and provider/platform

fields.name: is_certification
fields.description: True if this skill represents a certification

entities.name: SkillShortlist
entities.description: Shortlisted skills selected from the Skills page

fields.name: skill_ids
fields.description: Array of Skill.id values added to the shortlist

entities.name: BlogTag
entities.description: Tags used to classify blog posts (e.g., DevOps, Kubernetes, Monitoring)

entities.name: BlogPost
entities.description: Blog posts listed on the Blog page and readable in detail view

entities.name: ReadingList
entities.description: Saved/bookmarked blog posts added to the reading list

fields.name: blog_post_ids
fields.description: Array of BlogPost.id values saved to the reading list

entities.name: ExperienceRole
entities.description: Roles in the career timeline, filterable by role type (DevOps, SRE, etc.)

fields.name: order_index
fields.description: Optional manual sort index if not using dates

entities.name: RecruiterEvaluation
entities.description: Recruiter notes and evaluation fields shared across project and experience pages

fields.name: most_relevant_project
fields.description: Free-text project title copied into 'Most relevant project' field

fields.name: most_relevant_project_id
fields.description: Optional reference to Project.id for the most relevant project

fields.name: earliest_devops_sre_year
fields.description: Four-digit year of earliest DevOps/SRE role

entities.name: ResumeConfiguration
entities.description: Configuration for the tailored resume builder and print view

fields.name: generated_at
fields.description: Timestamp when resume was last generated/updated

fields.name: last_viewed_at
fields.description: Timestamp when print view was last opened

entities.name: ContactMessage
entities.description: Messages submitted via the Contact form, including project references and interview date

fields.name: message
fields.description: Full message body, including project titles and date text

fields.name: related_project_ids
fields.description: Optional list of Project.id values mentioned in the message

fields.name: preferred_interview_date
fields.description: Parsed interview date if extracted from message

entities.name: NewsletterTopic
entities.description: Available newsletter topics that can be selected on the subscription form

entities.name: NewsletterSubscription
entities.description: Subscriptions created via the Newsletter page, including email, frequency, and topics
